summary: Dan Shapiro shares a resource he discovered that compiles dozens of DIY poker table building projects from woodworkers across the web. He notes the variety of designs and sizes available, covering different card games, and highlights that most include instructions and all have pictures. The post is a brief, enthusiastic recommendation for fellow woodworkers interested in building their own poker tables, with Shapiro humorously admitting he may be the last woodworker who hasn't built one yet.
key_points: A comprehensive collection of DIY poker table designs exists online, aggregated from dozens of woodworkers
The designs span multiple card games and come with instructions and photos
All designs are shared free of charge, embodying the open maker community spirit
money_quotes: Evidently I'm not the only woodworker who has contemplated building a poker table… actually, judging by this site, I may be the only one who hasn't done it yet.
Oh, and did I mention the designs are all free of charge?
key_insight: The maker community freely shares detailed build plans, making ambitious woodworking projects accessible to anyone willing to try.
